title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek public's help to identify a second suspect in a lemonade stand robbery in South Boston.
Surveillance photos show the suspect wearing black pants, a black hat, and carrying a black backpack.
A 14-year-old boy was previously arrested and charged related to the June 10 robbery incident.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is exploring his final NBA team options, with Celtics considered among potential destinations by Rich Paul.
Boston Celtics' Brad Stevens and Jayson Tatum were noted, but not top contenders, for LeBron's next move.
James' potential role in Boston could be point-forward, joining Celtics' newly revamped roster targeting NBA Finals.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticizes the Boston Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish likened Brown's trade to the controversial Luka Doncic trade by the Dallas Mavericks in 2025.
Despite disapproval, Parish hopes for positive future relations between Brown and the Celtics.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude and respect for Jaylen Brown on social media following Brown's trade to the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and draft picks, according to ESPN's Shams Charania.
Brown felt disrespected by the Celtics before his trade, despite not requesting to be dealt from Boston.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Boston Celtics and Neemias Queta have agreed to a four-year, $56 million contract extension through the 2030-31 season.
Queta, a seven-foot center, posted 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 1.3 blocks across 76 games last season for the Celtics.
Originally drafted by the Kings in 2021, Queta joined the Celtics on a two-way contract in September 2023 and has steadily improved.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates its 200th anniversary, making it America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The building, likely constructed in 1716 or 1717, previously housed a Revolutionary War-era newspaper and fine goods importer.
Union Oyster House serves over 1,500 guests daily, with clam chowder and oysters among its most popular menu items.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston's summer highlights include the Pops Fireworks, a World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston's Parade of Sail.
The Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular takes place July 4 with performances by Chance the Rapper and others.
Sail Boston on July 11 will feature over 60 tall ships, led by the USS Constitution, to celebrate America’s 250th anniversary.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, Boston's oldest restaurant, has hosted notable guests including six U.S. presidents like Kennedy and Obama.
Celebrities such as Al Pacino, Meryl Streep, and Tiger Woods have visited, as highlighted in a 2003 painting by Stan Kotzen.
The restaurant was frequented by figures across eras, offering famous diners like Matt Damon and Muhammad Ali its signature clam chowder.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year veteran of NH State Police, died unexpectedly at 41 in Greenland, NH.
Ross served in multiple units, recently as commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
A fund for Ross's family has been established, and visitation is set for July 6-7 in Hampton.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized the Boston Celtics for a 'lack of respect' after being traded to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brown expressed dissatisfaction with Celtics executive Brad Stevens' communication regarding the trade discussions.
The Celtics traded Brown to the Sixers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
